wine_bars.name: Enoteca Al Volto
wine_bars.wine_program: Over 1,000 bottles from Veneto and beyond; traditional cicchetti; founded 1936; one of Venice's oldest continuously operating wine bars
wine_bars.description: Founded in 1936 near the Rialto Bridge, Al Volto pours from a list of over 1,000 bottles with a strong Veneto backbone. Wooden-panelled interior plastered with wine labels; traditional cicchetti served alongside regional selections.
wine_bars.tip: Open 10:00-16:00 and 18:00-22:00; arrive early evening to find a spot at the bar before the tourist rush.

wine_bars.name: Vino Vero
wine_bars.wine_program: 600+ labels from small producers; Venice's first bar dedicated solely to natural wine (opened 2014); biodynamic and organic selections; Veneto, Friuli, and Champagne growers
wine_bars.description: Opened 2014 by Matteo and Mara Bartoli, Vino Vero was the first natural-wine-only bar in Venice. Over 600 labels from artisanal producers; creative cicchetti with unusual ingredients. Canal-side position on Fondamenta della Misericordia.
wine_bars.tip: Check the chalk-board for the daily col fondo Prosecco pour - usually a different producer each week.

wine_bars.name: Cantina Do Mori
wine_bars.wine_program: Traditional Venetian ombra (small pour) culture; cicchetti served all day; strong Veneto wine selection; founded 1462 - oldest bacaro in Venice
wine_bars.description: Venice's oldest bacaro, established 1462 and hidden in an alley near the Rialto. Double entrance from two narrow calle; an institution for ombre (small wine pours) with traditional cicchetti mini-sandwiches. Mon-Sat 8:00-19:30.
wine_bars.tip: Go at 11:00 when the freshest cicchetti are laid out; Sunday is closed.

wine_bars.name: La Cantina
wine_bars.address: Cannaregio 3689, Campo San Felice, 30121 Venezia, Italy
wine_bars.wine_program: Made-to-order cicchetti with premium ingredients; strong Veneto and Friuli wine list; outdoor campo seating; knowledgeable staff
wine_bars.description: Hidden corner of Campo San Felice in Cannaregio; La Cantina prepares all cicchetti to order using the highest-quality local ingredients. Cosy interior, outdoor campo seats, and staff who greet regulars as old friends. Phone +39 041 522 8258.
wine_bars.tip: The tartare cicchetti changes daily depending on what arrived at the Rialto fish market that morning.

wine_bars.name: Cantine del Vino Gia Schiavi
wine_bars.wine_program: 500+ labels predominantly Italian; standing-only historic bacaro; Mon-Sat 8:30-20:30; also a retail wine shop
wine_bars.description: Known as 'Al Bottegon', this historic Dorsoduro bacaro dates to 1800. Standing-only wine bar and enoteca with 500+ labels from Italian producers. Sandwich cicchetti prepared to order at lunch. A neighbourhood institution beloved by locals and wine lovers.
wine_bars.tip: Arrive at 12:30 for the best sandwich selection before the lunch crowd depletes the board.

wine_bars.name: Estro Vino e Cucina
wine_bars.wine_program: 700+ natural wines from Europe; Michelin Guide Good Cooking 2025; contemporary Venetian cuisine with Japanese inflections
wine_bars.description: Opened 2014 by brothers Alberto and Dario Spezzamonte at the Dorsoduro / San Polo boundary. MICHELIN Guide Good Cooking designation 2025. Over 700 ecologically sustainable wines from small European producers; cicchetti and full dinner menu.
wine_bars.tip: Closed Tuesdays; open Mon and Wed-Sat 11:00-midnight.

wine_bars.name: Osteria del Bugiardo
wine_bars.wine_program: Exclusive Buglioni winery list - Valpolicella, Ripasso, Amarone, sparkling; house wines at accessible prices; lively Roman-street setting
wine_bars.description: Wine bar and osteria on Verona's ancient Corso Porta Borsari owned by Buglioni winery. Exclusive Buglioni list at affable prices; simple cicchetti, charcuterie, and cheeses. Sun-Thu 11:00-midnight, Fri-Sat 11:00-01:00.
wine_bars.tip: The Amarone by the glass is a rare bargain - Buglioni keeps house pricing far below comparable Amarone bars in Verona.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Dal Zovo
wine_bars.wine_program: Family-run since 1958; deep cellar; sommelier-guided selections on request; third-generation Mattia Dal Zovo at helm; Veneto and international list
wine_bars.description: Established 1958, now in its third generation with Mattia Dal Zovo. Situated across the Adige from Verona's historic centre on fashionable Via IV Novembre. Ask to see the cellar and the sommelier will guide selections from the deep Veneto and Italian list.
wine_bars.tip: The cellar visit is free for serious wine-seekers - just ask Mattia.

wine_bars.name: Enoteca del Soave
wine_bars.wine_program: Soave Classico and abroad, Garganega-Trebbiano and pure Garganega; Valpolicella; Lessini Durello classic method; curated charcuterie boards
wine_bars.description: Founded 1980 in the medieval village of Soave; returned to direct management by Cantina del Castello in April 2024. The best point-of-access for Soave and Valpolicella wines by the glass, with private room available for groups upstairs. Contact enotecasoave@gmail.com.
wine_bars.tip: Order the Lessini Durello metodo classico - this Veneto sparkling made from Durella grapes is one of the region's best-kept secrets.

wine_bars.name: Antica Bottega del Vino
wine_bars.address: Vicolo Scudo di Francia 3, 37121 Verona (VR), Italy
wine_bars.wine_program: Wine Spectator Grand Award winner since 2004; extensive Italian and international cellar; sommelier on duty daily; 11:00-01:00 Mon-Sun
wine_bars.description: Verona institution combining restaurant, wine bar, and wine cellar. Wine Spectator Grand Award recipient since 2004; cellar deep in Italian and international labels. Daily from 11:00 to 01:00; serious wine list with knowledgeable sommelier team.
wine_bars.tip: The private cellar tour (ask in advance) shows historic vintages going back to the 1940s.

verified.address_quoted: Vicolo Scudo di Francia, 3 - 37121 Verona VR

wine_bars.name: I Rusteghi
wine_bars.address: Corte del Tentor, San Marco 5513, 30124 Venezia, Italy
wine_bars.wine_program: Sommelier-run oasis in a quiet courtyard behind Rialto; curated Veneto and Friuli list; wine leads, food plays support; focused ombra selection
wine_bars.description: Hidden in a quiet courtyard just behind the Rialto in San Marco, I Rusteghi is a sommelier-run bacaro where the wine list takes priority. Strong Veneto and Friuli backbone with thoughtful cicchetti pairings. A locals' favourite for serious ombre.
wine_bars.tip: The courtyard is easy to miss - look for the small sign at the mouth of Calle del Tentor.

verified.address_quoted: Corte del Tentor, San Marco 5513, 30124 Venezia

wine_bars.name: Enoteca Baraldi
wine_bars.wine_program: Organic and sustainably produced wine focus; knowledgeable owner Bruno; Verona region specialties; retail and by-the-glass service
wine_bars.description: Family-owned enoteca in Verona's historic centre led by owner Bruno Baraldi, known for his organic and sustainably farmed selections with deep local knowledge. Also offers wine tastings through the Winedering platform. A retail and tasting hybrid.
wine_bars.tip: Ask Bruno about biodynamic Valpolicella producers - he sources directly from smaller estates not widely distributed.

wine_bars.name: Cantina del Castello
wine_bars.wine_program: Soave Classico DOC from medieval village cellar; cellar in 13th-century Counts Sambonifacio palace; Mon-Fri 8:00-18:30, Sat 10:00-17:00
wine_bars.description: Located at the foot of Soave Castle in the 13th-century Palazzo dei Conti Sambonifacio, Cantina del Castello has produced Soave Classico since the 1960s. Also operates the Enoteca del Soave retail outlet. Direct estate tastings in the historic cellar.
wine_bars.tip: The Pressoni single-vineyard Soave shows the volcanic basalt character that distinguishes the Classico zone.

wine_bars.name: Millevini Enoteca
wine_bars.address: Ramo del Fontego dei Tedeschi, San Marco 5362, 30124 Venezia, Italy
wine_bars.wine_program: Sommelier Lorenzo Menegus curates Veneto and Friuli selection with international wines; one of Venice's best retail shops; natural and conventional labels
wine_bars.description: Founded 2001 near the Rialto by certified sommelier Lorenzo Menegus. Every bottle is personally tasted and selected by Lorenzo. Strong Veneto and Friuli backbone; also carries grappa, rum, whisky, gin, and balsamic. One of Venice's most acclaimed wine shops.
wine_bars.tip: Tell Lorenzo your flavour profile and budget - his personal recommendations consistently overdeliver.

verified.address_quoted: Ramo del Fontego dei Tedeschi Sestiere San Marco, 5362 - 30100 Venezia (VE)
